summary: 8 items surfaced from 23 qualifying stories; full comment-tree scan completed.

items.name: Warp
items.summary: Open-source terminal with customizable Agents workflows.
items.why_included: Commenters described the Agents layout and customization as genuinely useful.
items.evidence: One commenter said the Agents layout fits their workflow so well; others praised the recent open-source move and new controls.

items.name: kitty
items.summary: Fast terminal emulator commenters told others to check out.
items.why_included: A direct recommendation made it stand out from the terminal discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter simply said, “Check out kitty if you haven’t yet.”

items.name: Lazygit
items.source_story_title: I have officially retired from Emacs
items.summary: Terminal Git UI praised as a Magit replacement on remote hosts.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced it as the closest practical substitute for editor-bound Git workflows.
items.evidence: A commenter called Lazygit the closest thing they had seen and said they use it on remote hosts when Magit is too painful.

items.name: runs-on
items.source_story_title: GitHub Actions is the weakest link
items.summary: Managed GitHub Actions runners people said worked well for AWS instances.
items.why_included: It was explicitly recommended as a useful CI runner alternative.
items.evidence: One commenter said their team has been using runs-on and that it had been great for AWS instance runners.

items.name: LocalSend
items.source_story_title: Localsend: An open-source cross-platform alternative to AirDrop
items.summary: Cross-platform AirDrop-style file transfer app praised for reliability and speed.
items.why_included: Many commenters described it as their go-to local file-transfer app.
items.evidence: Commenters said they use LocalSend across devices, called it great and interoperable, and described it as fast and useful.

items.name: PairDrop
items.source_story_title: Localsend: An open-source cross-platform alternative to AirDrop
items.summary: Browser-based file sharing PWA commenters urged others to try.
items.why_included: It appeared as a direct “also try” recommendation in the LocalSend discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter told readers to also try PairDrop and described it as a polished PWA with WebRTC peer-to-peer sharing.

items.name: Veritasium video
items.source_story_title: ASML became the chokepoint for cutting-edge chips
items.summary: ASML/EUV explainer recommended for understanding lithography.
items.why_included: The discussion pointed people to a clear, accessible explainer.
items.evidence: Two commenters recommended the Veritasium video as a good way to grasp EUV lithography and the machine’s complexity.

items.name: Asianometry channel
items.source_story_title: ASML became the chokepoint for cutting-edge chips
items.summary: Deep ASML and chip-supply-chain videos commenters recommended.
items.why_included: It came up as a strong follow-up for deeper semiconductor coverage.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended Asianometry for ASML, TSMC, and chip-fab supply-chain videos.
